key_insight: A hybrid model combines sharp-interface heat transfer with diffuse-interface fluid dynamics to accurately capture melt pool behavior under rapid evaporation.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable prediction of laser-metal processing outcomes such as welding and laser powder bed fusion, where interface temperature strongly controls recoil pressure and surface tension forces.
method: Sharp-interface CutFEM solves the thermal field with high accuracy at the melt-gas boundary.
Level-set one-fluid diffuse-interface FEM captures multiphase flow with large density ratios.
Temperature-dependent interface forces are evaluated by extending the sharp thermal field into a narrow coupling region.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports second-order spatial convergence for the thermal model and about an order-of-magnitude accuracy gain over a purely diffuse-interface model on the same mesh.
watch_item: The coupled approach adds implementation complexity because sharp thermal and diffuse flow models must be consistently interfaced and stabilized.
paper_title: A hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach to accurately model melt pool dynamics with rapid evaporation in laser-based processing of metals
